name: SEC 13F Holdings
description: Institutional investor quarterly holdings from 13F filings. Parsed and normalized.
longDescription: SEC Form 13F is the quarterly snapshot of institutional investor (hedge funds, mutual funds, pensions) positions in US equities. This dataset provides fully parsed and normalized holdings data extracted from raw SEC EDGAR filings. Each record represents one line item (holding) from one institutional manager's 13F filing.
updateSchedule: Quarterly (within 45 days of quarter-end); historical backfills through 2000

fields.name: filing_date
fields.description: SEC filing date (within 45 days of quarter-end)

fields.name: quarter_end
fields.description: Quarter-end date for which holdings are reported

fields.name: cik
fields.description: Central Index Key; SEC unique identifier for filing company

fields.name: manager_name
fields.description: Institutional manager name as reported to SEC

fields.name: ticker
fields.description: Stock ticker symbol (may be null for non-US ADRs or delisted securities)

fields.name: security_name
fields.description: Full security name from EDGAR filing

fields.name: shares_held
fields.description: Number of shares held as of quarter-end

fields.name: value_usd
fields.description: Fair value in USD (thousands). Derived from filing; not actual purchase price.

fields.name: none_voting
fields.description: Shares with no voting authority (e.g., ADRs with restricted voting)

sla.freshness: Quarterly; data becomes available within 45 days of quarter-end
sla.revisionPolicy: 13F filings are amended only rarely. Amendments (Form 13F/A) published as separate records. Historical filings are immutable.

codeExamples.label: Python
codeExamples.code:
import requests
import pandas as pd

# Fetch Berkshire Hathaway's latest 13F holdings
response = requests.get(
    'https://api.sec-data.com/v1/form-13f',
    params={
        'cik': '0001067983',  # Berkshire Hathaway
        'limit': 1000
    },
    headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'}
)

holdings = response.json()
df = pd.DataFrame(holdings['results'])

# Calculate portfolio concentration
df['portfolio_weight'] = df['value_usd'] / df['value_usd'].sum()
df = df.sort_values('portfolio_weight', ascending=False)

print(f"Top 10 Holdings:")
print(df[['security_name', 'ticker', 'shares_held', 'portfolio_weight']].head(10))

codeExamples.label: SQL
codeExamples.code:
-- Find all hedge funds holding AAPL in the latest quarter
SELECT 
  manager_name,
  shares_held,
  value_usd,
  filing_date,
  quarter_end,
  CAST(value_usd AS FLOAT) / SUM(value_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY cik) * 100 as pct_of_portfolio
FROM sec_13f_holdings
WHERE ticker = 'AAPL'
  AND quarter_end = (
    SELECT MAX(quarter_end) FROM sec_13f_holdings
  )
ORDER BY value_usd DESC;

codeExamples.label: C#

empirical.uniqueEntities: 5247 institutional managers (current); 450M+ holdings lines (25 years history)

lineage.description: Form 13F filings submitted to SEC via EDGAR. Includes cover page, summary, and detailed holdings tables.
Extracts structured holdings data from SEC's XML submission and HTML renderings. Handles variations in formatting.
Maps CUSIP codes to tickers using SEC cross-reference and third-party databases (Bloomberg, FactSet). Handles delistings.
Checks filing totals, detects duplicate lines, validates CIK/manager relationships. Flags amended filings (13F/A).
For filings prior to 2012, normalizes field names and data types to current schema. Backfill verified back to 2000.
Publishes parsed data to REST API, bulk export CSV, and Snowflake Share. Quarterly updates triggered within 2 days of filing.

versionPolicy.deprecationPolicy: Current version supported indefinitely. Previous major versions supported for 10 years minimum (for historical research).
versionPolicy.breakingChanges: v3.0 (Mar 2020): Deprecated legacy 'ADS' flag; consolidated voting columns (sole_voting, shared_voting, none_voting). v2.0 (2012): Added CUSIP field and standardized date formats.
versionPolicy.revisionWindow: Form 13F/A (amended filings) published as separate records with same CIK/quarter. Original filing is never modified.

compliance.regulatoryClassification: Public regulatory filing data from SEC EDGAR. No restrictions on use (U.S. federal public domain).
compliance.dataRetentionPolicy: All filings from 1995-present retained. EDGAR archives go back to 1994 but earlier data less complete.

backtestGuide.sampleBacktest:
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Load Berkshire Hathaway's largest 10 positions over time
qtrs = ['2024-12-31', '2024-09-30', '2024-06-30', '2024-03-31']
portfolio_history = []

for qtr in qtrs:
    holdings = fetch_13f(cik='0001067983', quarter_end=qtr)
    top_10 = holdings.nlargest(10, 'value_usd')
    top_10['quarter_end'] = qtr
    portfolio_history.append(top_10)

df = pd.concat(portfolio_history)
print("Largest holdings by quarter:")
print(df.pivot_table(values='value_usd', index='security_name', columns='quarter_end'))
knownLimitations: Form 13F reports positions *as of quarter-end*, not at filing date. Data is 40+ days stale when published.
13F only covers equity holdings >$100K position value. Smaller positions are not disclosed.
Put/call options (put_call_flag) are treated as separate line items; aggregate stock+options positions separately.
Survivorship bias: liquidated funds disappear from dataset. Historical data skews toward survivors.
Multiple class shares (e.g., BRKA vs BRKB) may be reported as separate line items; requires aggregation for total exposure.
Non-US ADRs and foreign ordinaries may lack ticker mappings; rely on CUSIP for identification.
backtestGuide.lookbackRequired: 90 days minimum to identify manager portfolio weights. One year minimum for position turnover and conviction trends.
backtestGuide.warmupPeriod: Minimum 4-8 quarters (1-2 years) to calibrate baseline manager behavior; some strategies need 5+ years for regime identification

license: Public Domain. This data is extracted from SEC EDGAR filings which are U.S. federal public domain. No restrictions on redistribution or derived works.
